title: PTD Today — Media

items.title: Would you go to an in-person dating app event?

ai.summary: Based on the available description, Gen Z is increasingly struggling with loneliness and dating app Hinge is expanding its One More Hour social impact initiative. Hinge announced a $1 million fund to support social groups in New York, Los Angeles and London to put on free or affordable in-person events for young people.
bullets: Hinge launched a $1 million fund for social groups.
Funds target New York, Los Angeles and London.
Purpose is to support free or affordable in-person social events.
Part of Hinge’s global expansion of its One More Hour initiative.
Motivation tied to rising loneliness among Gen Z.
takeaways: Geographic focus (NY, LA, London) identifies metros where event-driven activity could increase local demand for services.
A $1M program aimed at Gen Z suggests concentrated, time-limited social initiatives rather than ongoing large-scale infrastructure projects.
Free or affordable in-person events imply grassroots, distributed gatherings—monitor localized service and connectivity needs around event venues.
The corporate-backed nature of the initiative (Hinge’s One More Hour) may produce predictable, scheduled spikes tied to promoted events—coordinate monitoring accordingly.

items.title: Build Knowledge Graph-powered AI Agents For Your Use Case | Databases for AI

ai.summary: Based on the available description… This session demonstrates how to automatically build a knowledge graph from raw documents and connect it to an AI agent so the agent can understand company-specific information. The end-to-end demo requires no prior graph expertise and shows how to make agents aware of your business data.
bullets: AI agents often lack knowledge of company-specific data; knowledge graphs address this gap.
The session builds a knowledge graph automatically from raw documents.
The graph is connected to an AI agent so it can answer questions about your business.
No graph queries or graph theory knowledge required; a full end-to-end demo is shown.
takeaways: So what: Knowledge graphs can make AI agents useful for internal, company-specific queries.
So what: Automated graph construction reduces the skill barrier for deploying graph-backed agents.
So what: An end-to-end workflow demonstrates a practical path to integrating business data with AI agents.
So what: This approach can accelerate internal adoption of AI that relies on organizational documents.

items.title: Leveraging tax credits and incentives for sustainability projects

ai.summary: Based on the available description… Randa Barsoum of PwC US emphasizes that energy incentives are only part of the tax landscape for sustainability. She highlights less obvious opportunities—state and local abatements, location-based incentives, and R&D tax credits tied to innovation (for example, reimagining packaging or processes)—and urges leaders to view sustainability investments through a broader tax lens to unlock meaningful value.
bullets: Energy incentives are common but not the full set of tax opportunities for sustainability.
State and local abatements can support sustainability projects.
Location-based incentives may be available depending on site.
R&D tax credits can apply to innovation efforts, such as reimagining packaging or processes.
Viewing sustainability investments broadly can unlock meaningful tax value.
takeaways: Include state and local abatements and location-based incentives in project evaluations and site-selection for grid/data center investments.
Consider whether sustainability-driven innovation in processes or operations qualifies for R&D tax credits.
Embed a broader tax-credit assessment into sustainability project planning to maximize potential tax value.

items.title: GeForce Garage - Summer of RTX Community Build

ai.summary: Based on the available description, GeForce Garage built the Summer of RTX r/buildapc Community Build contest winner — a small-form-factor PC by u/JeffersonPutnam featuring a GeForce RTX 5090 Founders Edition. The description lists the full parts list including a Fractal Design Era 2 chassis, MSI MPG B850I Edge motherboard, Ryzen 7 9800X3D, 32GB DDR5-6000, Corsair SF1000 PSU and Corsair Nautilus 240 RS AIO.
bullets: Contest winner: u/JeffersonPutnam — SFF GeForce RTX 5090 rig.
Host: GeForce host Dwight (Twitter: @_MightyPotato).
Full parts list available on PCPartPicker: https://pcpartpicker.com/list/hxnCRV.
Key components: Fractal Design Era 2, RTX 5090 Founders Edition, MSI MPG B850I Edge, Ryzen 7 9800X3D, 32GB DDR5-6000, Corsair SF1000, Corsair Nautilus 240 RS.
Published by NVIDIA GeForce with social links and community portal references.
takeaways: High-end GPU in an SFF build highlights the need to account for power delivery (Corsair SF1000) when integrating flagship accelerators.
Use of a 240mm AIO (Corsair Nautilus 240 RS) points to liquid cooling as a practical thermal solution in compact enclosures.
System balance (Ryzen 7 9800X3D and 32GB DDR5-6000) underscores matching CPU and memory specifications to complement high-performance GPUs.
Chassis choice (Fractal Design Era 2) illustrates spatial and compatibility considerations when deploying large GPUs in constrained form factors.

items.title: TTEC accelerates AI deployment and revenue streams with Google Cloud Consulting

ai.summary: Based on the available description, TTEC partnered with Google Cloud Consulting to rapidly prototype and deploy AI solutions that unlocked new revenue streams. Sreeni Alakappan explains how leveraging GenAI and Google Cloud's robust infrastructure streamlined operations, boosted developer productivity, and compressed the company's digital transformation timeline.
bullets: TTEC worked with Google Cloud Consulting to accelerate AI prototyping and deployment.
GenAI and Google Cloud infrastructure were used to streamline operations and improve developer productivity.
The engagement enabled TTEC to compress its digital transformation timeline.
New AI-powered capabilities opened up entirely new revenue streams for the business.
Google Cloud Consulting helped expand TTEC’s strategic ambitions for customer experience tech.
takeaways: Robust cloud infrastructure is critical to support GenAI workloads and fast deployment.
Partnering with cloud consulting can compress transformation timelines and reduce rollout risk.
Improved developer productivity shortens time-to-market, affecting capacity and scaling needs.
AI-driven operational efficiencies and new revenue streams can justify cloud investment.
Streamlined operations may lower operational complexity and influence data center strategy.
